title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. abyssinica
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. abyssinica, also known as the Abyssinian kidney vetch, is a subspecies of the kidney vetch plant, a flowering perennial native to the mountainous regions of Ethiopia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. The Abyssinian kidney vetch has been traditionally used to treat fever, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, making it a potential treatment for various diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, or other medicinal products.
